name: Bryan Stevenson
practice: Death penalty defense, racial justice litigation, narrative memorialization
approach: Each of us is more than the worst thing we've ever done. The system works by dehumanizing — turning people into cases, numbers, sentences. Justice requires proximity: you must be close enough to the condemned to see them as human. Mercy is not weakness — it is the measure of our justice.
body_of_work: Founded Equal Justice Initiative. Won Supreme Court rulings banning mandatory life-without-parole for children. Just Mercy (book and film). National Memorial for Peace and Justice — the first memorial to lynching victims. Freed over 140 wrongly condemned prisoners.
why_instructive: The dome's legal layer must reckon with the criminal justice system's ability to destroy every other layer simultaneously — an arrest collapses housing, employment, health, community, education. Stevenson shows what repair looks like.

dome_relevance.1: Legal — criminal defense, wrongful conviction, death penalty abolition
dome_relevance.4: Health — mental health in incarceration, trauma of the condemned
dome_relevance.6: Economic — employment barriers, economic destruction of conviction
dome_relevance.8: Community — community impact of mass incarceration

sphere_relevance.5: Social — the communities mass incarceration devastates
sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — lynching memorial makes invisible history visible in physical space
sphere_relevance.9: Policy — sentencing reform, juvenile justice reform
